TITLE: AI AND MACHINE LEARNING: A RETROSPECTIVE

[THESIS]
	In recent years, the term “AI” has been thrown around countless times in the emergence and persistence of powerful LLMs and diffusion-based image generation tools. Ask anyone about what AI is and they’ll probably talk about ChatGPT, Claude, Stable Diffusion, DALL-E, and other popular models depending on their occupation and background. But AI isn’t just that. AI is a lot more than the generative tools we use today; it isn’t just the text generator on OpenAI’s webserver client we see, or the generated artwork that is garnering outrage as it circulates through X, facebook, and other social media of the like. Nor is it the weird, surreal advertisements shown on television or list of soundtracks from unnamed artists making big names in the billboards. Those things are only a small fraction of a much larger, and much more complicated rabbit hole.
	To better understand this, we have to look at AI not as a tool, but as a visual display of decades of technical design and innovation. A lot of its workings and underpinnings lie under the hood: in the cloud service data centers it currently exists in, in someone’s local machine, and in old antiquated tech that hasn’t seen the light in ages. This is what ends up creating the outputs that end up everywhere on our feed, or the “visual component” of the technology. I want to expose those beautiful hidden mechanisms that quietly power our command line interfaces (CLIs), chatbots, video games, social media, and more. 
	As mentioned, the AI we know today isn’t a spontaneous spark of technological advancement that came out of nowhere; the ubiquitous term of “AI” actually belongs to decades of brilliantly designed algorithms, digital architecture, and interfaces that have long existed within our systems even before the whole idea became mainstream. In fact, AI has already been used and integrated into many of our digital systems and technology in ways that we wouldn’t have imagined it had — more on that later . As such, we’ll examine the historical underpinnings and concept of machine learning, break down AI architecture and developments over the years, and unfold the good and the ugly of AI in the current age.
